item.title: How Trump became tech’s regulator-in-chief
item.description: His interventions in the sector exceed anything the EU has done

item.title: Orbán faces an electoral reckoning in Hungary
item.description: Defeat for the populist rightwing premier would reverberate across Europe

item.title: Stagflation fears demolish confidence in UK housing
item.description: Shares across the sector are taking a beating

item.title: How we gave up on forgiveness
item.description: What was once seen as a virtue is now viewed as a moral weakness — leaving a society mired in toxicity

item.title: Good news for CEOs with foreign accents
item.description: Investors may prefer them but sadly the benefit does not extend to the rest of us

item.title: Populists will regret doing God
item.description: A movement founded on loucheness is making an electorally risky turn towards religion

item.title: Trump’s tales are still muddling markets
item.description: Continuation of the Middle East war is sapping hopes of a return to more normal conditions after the conflict

item.title: Sacred pilgrimage has journeyed to commercial boom
item.description: Forget the image of medieval piety — it’s a very modern part of the travel industry

item.title: Osteria Vibrato, London: Pasta, a piano and a note of magic — restaurant review
item.description: It’ll make you nostalgic for a Soho you never knew

item.title: For Easter-egg makers, it is chocolate tomorrow
item.description: Sweeter times lie ahead as the market for cocoa buyers improves but prices take time to feed through the chain

item.title: Trump’s architectural vices laid bare in the Miami library plan
item.description: The presidential library design takes POTUS showboating to a new, but still gold-plated, level

item.title: Resurrection as an ongoing practice
item.description: Easter is a good time to think about how we awaken ourselves to new possibilities

item.title: Former CIA operative: regime change in Iran is much harder than the US thinks
item.description: Covert action can help topple a leader but it rarely builds legitimacy

item.title: What’s the point of an AI novel?
item.description: The danger is not that it will replace human-authored books — but that we stop caring about good writing at all

item.title: How can two bottles of the same wine taste so different?
item.description: Only the very cheapest wine offers total consistency

item.title: The great Wall Street relocation that never quite happens
item.description: While Apollo seeks a second headquarters in the American south, demand for office space rises in New York

item.title: It’s far too easy to get sucked down a conspiracy theory rabbit hole
item.description: We all tend to accept information that is consistent with our prior beliefs, and reject the opposite

item.title: Forget Trump, I’m now fully invested again
item.description: Why right now seems like the right time for me to move out of cash

item.title: An inspiring new race to the Moon
item.description: Artemis II is reawakening interest in science and the thrill of human exploration

item.title: Management consultants are ruining UK universities
item.description: Relentless off-the-peg commercial rewiring has undermined British higher education

item.title: What’s so sexy about vampires?
item.description: Yes, there’s the thrill of forbidden love and Robert Pattinson. But there’s also the bit where your throat gets ripped out

item.title: Trust fund battle shows the limits of the UK’s retail investing culture
item.description: If policymakers really want an active retail investing culture, they should remove the frictions

item.title: What’s going on with dollar debt?
item.description: ‘Swap’ trades by hedge funds have exploded recently

item.title: Preventive wars have weakened America
item.description: Resorting to force when there is time for diplomacy is needlessly destabilising and tends to end in failure

item.title: Top tips for Easter planting
item.description: Avoid the traps: don’t be fooled into buying what’s in flower now. Impatience comes with a huge price tag
